source_pr.title: pkg/privilege: fix flaky ldap TLS tests
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #63458

Problem Summary:
- LDAP TLS tests were flaky due to random port collisions / listen races when picking a port manually.

### What changed and how does it work?
- Use `localhost:0` and propagate listen errors; derive the actual port from the listener.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Test Details:
- `go test -run ^TestConnectWithTLS11$ -count=50 --tags=intest ./pkg/privilege/privileges/ldap`
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: pkg/privilege: fix flaky ldap TLS tests
failure_signature: pkg/privilege: fix flaky ldap TLS tests
root_cause_explanation: LDAP TLS tests previously picked random ports, which can conflict with other processes and fail intermittently. This PR binds to `localhost:0` (OS-assigned free port), propagates listener startup errors, and reads back the chosen port, eliminating port-collision flakiness.
fix_pattern: Fix test synchronization ordering (WaitGroup/channel/goroutine timing) to eliminate race-driven flakes.

evidence: evidence: pkg/privilege: fix flaky ldap TLS tests
